name: Beogradski Sindikat
description: '''Beogradski Sindikat''' ({{lang-sr-Cyrl|Београдски синдикат|lit=Belgrade Syndicate}}) is a [[Serbian hip-hop]] collective from [[Belgrade]], [[Serbia]]. Beogradski Sindikat is one of the pioneers of the second wave of Serbian [[hip-hop]]. The band is known for its socially critical lyrics, especially in relation to [[political corruption]], [[propaganda]] and bad governance in Serbia and the [[Balkans]]. Other main themes are the idealization of friendly togetherness, a sense of belonging to the city of Belgrade, and patriotism towards [[Serbian culture]] and the [[Serbian Orthodox Church]], as well as the rejection of foreign political influence on Serbia. Due to their critical nature, [[Milo Đukanović]], the [[president of Montenegro]], called the group's lyrics an insult to honor.
